vocabulary.meaning: defeat an opponent in a game
vocabulary.example: Our team beat them 3-2.

vocabulary.meaning: failure to win a game or battle
vocabulary.example: She admitted defeat after a long match.

vocabulary.meaning: game played on a board with pieces
vocabulary.example: Monopoly is a very popular board game.

vocabulary.meaning: leader of a sports team or ship
vocabulary.example: Harry is the captain of the football club.

vocabulary.meaning: something difficult that tests your ability
vocabulary.example: Learning English is a big challenge.

vocabulary.meaning: winner of a competition
vocabulary.example: She is the new chess champion.

vocabulary.meaning: be dishonest to win or get an advantage
vocabulary.example: He was caught cheating in the exam.

vocabulary.meaning: traditional orchestral music
vocabulary.example: He loves listening to classical music like Beethoven.

vocabulary.meaning: organization for people with shared interests
vocabulary.example: I recently joined a local sports club.

vocabulary.meaning: person who trains an athlete or team
vocabulary.example: The coach gave us some useful advice.

vocabulary.meaning: event where people try to win
vocabulary.example: He won first prize in the competition.

vocabulary.meaning: live musical performance
vocabulary.example: We bought tickets for the pop concert.

vocabulary.meaning: interesting and enjoyable
vocabulary.example: The film was very entertaining.

vocabulary.meaning: traditional music of a country or culture
vocabulary.example: They played traditional folk music.

vocabulary.meaning: number of people or things together
vocabulary.example: A group of students stood outside.

vocabulary.meaning: room or building for physical exercise
vocabulary.example: I go to the gym twice a week.

vocabulary.meaning: enjoy yourself!
vocabulary.example: Have fun at the party tonight!

vocabulary.meaning: want to know or learn about something
vocabulary.example: Do you have any interest in sports?

vocabulary.meaning: person belonging to a group or club
vocabulary.example: She is a member of the library.

vocabulary.meaning: person you play or fight against
vocabulary.example: He defeated his opponent in the final.

vocabulary.meaning: plan or arrange an event
vocabulary.example: We need to organise a welcome party.

vocabulary.meaning: feeling of happiness or enjoyment
vocabulary.example: It's a pleasure to meet you.

vocabulary.meaning: official who controls a sports match
vocabulary.example: The referee blew his whistle to stop the match.

vocabulary.meaning: regular pattern of sounds or movements
vocabulary.example: The drum has a fast rhythm.

vocabulary.meaning: possibility of something bad happening
vocabulary.example: Starting a business is a big risk.

vocabulary.meaning: number of points won in a game
vocabulary.example: What's the final score of the game?

vocabulary.meaning: help or encourage someone
vocabulary.example: Thank you for your support.

vocabulary.meaning: group of people playing or working together
vocabulary.example: We work well as a team.

vocabulary.meaning: practice a sport or skill
vocabulary.example: Athletes train hard every day.

vocabulary.meaning: game played on a screen or console
vocabulary.example: He spends hours playing video games.

vocabulary.meaning: game where players throw a ball through a hoop
vocabulary.example: They are playing basketball in the yard.

vocabulary.meaning: small tool that makes a high sound when blown
vocabulary.example: The referee blew his whistle.

vocabulary.meaning: entertain an audience by acting, singing, or dancing
vocabulary.example: The band will perform live tonight.

vocabulary.meaning: outdoor event with games, rides, and stalls
vocabulary.example: We bought some toys at the local fair.

vocabulary.meaning: extremely clever or bright
vocabulary.example: She had a brilliant idea.

vocabulary.meaning: jumping from a plane wearing a parachute
vocabulary.example: He made a parachute jump from the plane.

vocabulary.meaning: something you hope to achieve
vocabulary.example: My dream is to travel around the world.

vocabulary.meaning: group of people who govern an area
vocabulary.example: The local council decided to build a new gym.

vocabulary.meaning: comfortable chairs with sides to rest your arms
vocabulary.example: He sat comfortably in one of the armchairs.

vocabulary.meaning: have enough money to buy something
vocabulary.example: I can't afford to buy that expensive car.

vocabulary.meaning: person who does not eat meat
vocabulary.example: She is a vegetarian and doesn't eat meat.

vocabulary.meaning: flesh of an animal used as food
vocabulary.example: I don't eat much red meat.

vocabulary.meaning: large sports ground with seats around it
vocabulary.example: The stadium was packed with football fans.

vocabulary.meaning: extremely good or wonderful
vocabulary.example: We had a fantastic time at the park.

vocabulary.meaning: hit your hands together to show approval
vocabulary.example: The audience clapped for the actors.

vocabulary.meaning: doing a job or sport for money
vocabulary.example: He is a professional football player.

vocabulary.meaning: telling the truth; not lying
vocabulary.example: Please be honest with me.

vocabulary.meaning: run after someone or something to catch them
vocabulary.example: The dog chased the cat up the tree.

vocabulary.meaning: young cat
vocabulary.example: Our cat has just had four cute kittens.

vocabulary.meaning: sport of riding a skateboard
vocabulary.example: Skateboarding is popular among teenagers.

phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Carry on with your work while I am away.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: eat at a restaurant instead of home
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We decided to eat out tonight.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop doing a habit or activity
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He gave up smoking last year.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: participate or take part in an activity
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Ask them if we can join in the game.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: make a player leave a game for breaking rules
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The player was sent off for a bad foul.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start a new hobby or sport
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I've taken up painting recently.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: reduce the volume; reject an offer
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Could you turn down the music? It's too loud.
EX: Turn up the radio, I love this song!

prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: I haven't seen her for a long time.
EX: I only play games for fun.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: at the central point of something
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He stood in the middle of the room.
EX: We arrived just in time for the film.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: recorded on a disk or tape
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Is the movie released on DVD yet?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: performing in front of an audience
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The actors are performing on stage.

wordFormation.meaning: act: perform or take action / active: busy doing things / inactive: not doing anything / actor or actress: person who performs in a play or movie
wordFormation.example:
EX: He acted very well in the play.
EX: She's an active member of the club.
EX: My favorite actor is Johnny Depp.

wordFormation.meaning: athlete: person who plays sports / athletic: strong and good at sports / athletics: sports like running and jumping
wordFormation.example:
EX: The athlete won a gold medal.
EX: She has an athletic build.
EX: Athletics is popular at school.

wordFormation.meaning: children: more than one child / childhood: the time when you are a child
wordFormation.example:
EX: The children are playing in the park.
EX: I had a very happy childhood.

wordFormation.meaning: collection: group of similar objects / collector: person who collects things
wordFormation.example:
EX: She has a collection of old coins.
EX: He is a stamp collector.

wordFormation.meaning: entertain: invite people and give them food or fun / entertainment: shows, films, or games that interest people
wordFormation.example:
EX: He entertained us with his stories.
EX: There is live entertainment tonight.

wordFormation.meaning: heroic: very brave / hero: brave man or main male character / heroine: brave woman or main female character
wordFormation.example:
EX: He made a heroic rescue attempt.
EX: The hero of the movie is a young detective.
EX: She is a national heroine.

wordFormation.meaning: musical: relating to music / musician: person who plays or writes music
wordFormation.example:
EX: He plays a musical instrument.
EX: She is a talented musician.

wordFormation.meaning: player: person who plays a game / playful: full of fun and wanting to play
wordFormation.example:
EX: He is a tennis player.
EX: The puppy is very playful.

wordFormation.meaning: sailing: sport of using boats with sails / sailor: person who works on a ship
wordFormation.example:
EX: They went sailing in the lake.
EX: The sailor steered the ship.

wordFormation.meaning: sing: make musical sounds with the voice / singer: person who sings / singing: activity of making musical sounds with the voice
wordFormation.example:
EX: She loves to sing pop songs.
EX: He is a famous singer.
EX: I heard singing next door.

wordPatterns.example: EX: The boys are bored with staying at home all day.

wordPatterns.meaning: very excited or enthusiastic about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Tom is crazy about football.
EX: Helen is good at Maths (doing Maths).

wordPatterns.meaning: wanting to learn or know more about something

wordPatterns.example: EX: I am keen on reading books.

wordPatterns.meaning: liked by a specific group of people
wordPatterns.example: EX: This game is popular with teenagers.

wordPatterns.meaning: want to have or do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: I feel like a complete idiot.

wordPatterns.meaning: pay attention to sounds or music
wordPatterns.example: EX: I'm fond of listening to folk music.

wordPatterns.meaning: join or participate in an event
wordPatterns.example: EX: She wants to take part in the competition.

wordPatterns.meaning: written work describing a specific topic
wordPatterns.example: EX: He wrote a book about chess.

wordPatterns.meaning: person who admires someone or something greatly
wordPatterns.example: EX: I am a fan of classical music.

wordPatterns.meaning: a match played in competition with another side
wordPatterns.example: EX: We played a game against the champions.
